turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Okay. But I don't want to get to what, accepting a contribution for an election?

STEELE; The solicitation is permitted in revolves around the question of whether they are members QUESTION* And the exception to that MB. STEELE* Yes. So that the critical the definition of members as intended by Congress. And what purpose does that serve. They felt that there was no valid purpose there. Again, the underlying purpose I would note, as I started to, that there is a political action committee. QUESTION* Mr. Steele, is this the precise presidential and vice — and if not, what is? MS. STEELE* That is the operative language. throughout the history of the statute, as prohibiting the that statute could be read or should be read to prohibit expenditures that were made for the purpose of a stock corporation. So that that phrase, by that interpretation is clearly meant not to allow a corporation to make QUESTION* Mr. Steele, I suppose there could Congress could, for instance, just prohibit the ME. STEELE* I think that that could be done. I think the question would be somewhat is that less restrictive or more restrictive, because if you had a dollar amount that might not reflect the size of the corporation. So I think that Congress, in dealing with that question, dealt with it in somewhat different terms, but basically dealt specifically with the expenditures by the corporation for those kinds of expenses.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Well, they could have left the statute the way it was, before 76. HR. STEELE: They could have. Before 76, — I mean, in 76 what they felt was — at least with regard -

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: That corporations should be able to spend soma of its money for soliciation, subject to some limitation. HR. STEELE: But that solicitation was only for its —

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Yes. HR. STEELE: Its shareholders. I agree Congress could have left the statute as it was.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: We'll resume there are 1:00 o'clock. And would you, at least for me, address the Bellotti case at that time.
